message:
Running command: 
# OpenROAD syscall = "running" (not blocked on write), CPU accumulating - GOOD!
# The Python drainer is keeping the pipe drained.
# 30000 groups completed - progressing well.
# 
# Now let's do periodic monitoring while the Python drainer keeps the pipe clear.

for i in $(seq 1 120); do
    sleep 30
    ORPID=$(ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
    [ -z "$ORPID" ] && echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] OpenROAD exited!" && break
    
    SYSCALL=$(cat /proc/$ORPID/syscall 2>/dev/null | head -1)
    CPUTIME=$(ps -p $ORPID -o cputime= 2>/dev/null)
    PIPEBYTES=$(wc -c < /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null)
    LATESTMSG=$(tail -3 /tmp/drt2_pipe.log 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' '|')
    
    RUN_DIR="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
    FINAL=$([ -d "$RUN_DIR/final" ] && echo "YES" || echo "no")
    LATEST_STEP=$(ls "$RUN_DIR/" | sort -V | grep "^[0-9]" | tail -1)
    
    echo "[$i/120 $(date +%H:%M:%S)] OR=$ORPID SYSCALL=$SYSCALL CPU=$CPUTIME PIPE=${PIPEBYTES}B FINAL=$FINAL STEP=$LATEST_STEP | $LATESTMSG"
    
    [ "$FINAL" = "YES" ] && echo "=== FLOW COMPLETE ===" && break
done

function.name: execute_bash
